subject: Merge SDNR change to sdnc startODL script
commit_message:
Merge SDNR change to sdnc startODL script

Add SDNR feature and adjust netconf for cluster

Change-Id: I6c9063e6726690923fc77d58625113c1eb83a561
Issue-ID: SDNC-721
Signed-off-by: Herbert Eiselt <herbert.eiselt@highstreet-technologies.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Build Started https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/1883/

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/1883/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/oom-master-verify-helm/1883

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

This looks good - thanks!

Patch Set 1:

Gitlab Orange: Starting Chained-CI build

Patch Set 1:

Gitlab Orange: Chained-CI build started, max time: 10800 seconds

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

odl-netconf-topology and odl-netconf-clustered-topology should not be installed together.They cause problems with clustering,as mentioned in the odl documentation.In the clustering case please remove odl-netconf-topology and only keep odl-netconf-clustered-topology.From ODL Documentation:https://docs.opendaylight.org/en/stable-oxygen/user-guide/netconf-user-guide.html

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

Odl-netconf-topology and odl-netconf-clustered-topology should not be run together as they cause clustering(netconf) to fail this is documented in odl docs and we have tested the same as well. https://docs.opendaylight.org/en/stable-oxygen/user-guide/netconf-user-guide.html
Removed Code-Review-1 by Rahul Yadav <rahul4talk@gmail.com>

(1 comment)

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

SDNC healthcheck is OK and there's no sdnc pods in the failing list
Removed Code-Review+1 by Rahul Yadav <rahul4talk@gmail.com>

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)
Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

The function is replacing according to the name. See implementation in line 46: sed -i "/featuresBoot/ s/$1/$2/g"

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 1:

According to SDNC-555 odl-netconf-topology was included into the sdnc base container. So odl-netconf-topology comes with the container. For cluster configuration it is replaced by the script to odl-netconf-cluster-topology.

Patch Set 1:

In the actual (since yesterday) sdnc gerrit Dockerfile the odl-netconf-connector-all was already removed. But it was in the container in the previouse version. 
My tests with the SDNC container with the gerrit/source was successfull and there was always only one netconf-topology active. I verified this. 
Perhaps the scipt should handle also removing of odl-netconf-connector-all.

Patch Set 1:

If you install both odl-netconf-connector-all(which contains odl-netconf-topology) and odk-netconf-clustered-topology(which contains odl-netconf-topology as well).That now adds the plugin two times and causes the netconf clustering to dail as has been tested.
Please make sure odl-netconf-connector-all is not present in the final image.
Odl documentation clearly states that you cannot have both the features and this will cause the netconf clustering to fail

Patch Set 1:

Please make sure that the script removes odl-netconf-connector-all,if it is present

Patch Set 1:

Ok will abandon his change a provide a new one with the requested extension to remove odl-netconf-connector-all if found in boot features.

Patch Set 1:

In deed the sdnc image from 0404 includes this "old" featuresBoot = ee197ccf-3af7-4adc-a047-5eb910fc5525,odl-restconf-all,odl-netconf-connector-all,odl-netconf-clustered-topology,odl-mdsal-all,odl-mdsal-apidocs, odl-daexim-all, odl-restconf-nb-rfc804  ..

Abandoned

Extend script to remove "odl-netconf-connector-all" to submit again.

Patch Set 1:

Thanks Herbert ,Please make sure you do submit the new changes before the code freeze today as you have already modified the changes in sdnc dockerfile and if the startODL.sh is not updated the netconf clustering functionality will be broken, it was working fine before your changes.
